api/client/python.purpose: Public Python SDK for OpenMeter — a thin shim layer re-exporting from _generated/ sub-packages with a stable versioned surface. The primary constraint is that all business logic and operations must come from _generated/ only; this folder just wires them together.

patterns.name: Thin shim over _generated
patterns.description: openmeter/__init__.py and _client.py re-export from _generated/; no new operation logic is added here.
patterns.example: from openmeter._generated import OpenMeterClient; class Client(OpenMeterClient): ...

patterns.name: _patch_sdk() at module end
patterns.description: _patch_sdk() must be called at the end of __init__.py to apply runtime patches from _patch.py; removing or moving it silently disables patches.

patterns.name: Graceful _patch ImportError guard
patterns.description: Imports of _patch symbols must be wrapped in try/except ImportError so the SDK still works when _patch.py is absent.

patterns.name: Poetry for build and publish
patterns.description: scripts/release.sh computes PEP-440 version from live PyPI, stamps _version.py/_commit.py, then runs poetry publish --build in a single step.

patterns.name: Environment-variable client configuration
patterns.description: Examples must read endpoint from OPENMETER_ENDPOINT and token from OPENMETER_TOKEN; no hardcoded values.

patterns.name: Sync/async example symmetry
patterns.description: examples/sync/ and examples/async/ are structurally identical; sync uses openmeter.Client, async uses openmeter.aio.Client exclusively.

patterns.name: __all__ extended from _patch_all
patterns.description: __all__ in __init__.py must be extended from _patch_all to stay forward-compatible when new symbols are patched in.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the public Client class subclassing the generated OpenMeterClient; only extension points, no new operations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding new operation groups directly here instead of in _generated/.

key_file_guides.role: Public package surface; re-exports Client, models, and operations. Must call _patch_sdk() at end.
Union type aliases using string forward references; keeps heavy type imports out of __init__.py.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Using runtime (non-TYPE_CHECKING) imports of heavy generated types.

key_file_guides.role: Written transiently by scripts/release.sh; not committed.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Committing this file — it is regenerated on every publish run.

key_file_guides.role: Written transiently by scripts/release.sh with the git SHA; not committed.
Sole publish script; queries PyPI for next alpha version, stamps version files, then calls poetry publish --build.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PY_SDK_RELEASE_TAG values other than 'alpha' hard-error; PY_SDK_RELEASE_VERSION with leading 'v' may produce invalid PEP-440 strings.

key_file_guides.role: Poetry project definition; Python >=3.9, corehttp[requests,aiohttp] >=1.0.0b7, cloudevents ^1.12.1.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Changing version manually here — release.sh overwrites it at publish time.

key_file_guides.role: PEP-561 marker file enabling type checking for downstream consumers; must be present in the installed package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Removing from MANIFEST.in or package data — breaks Pyright/mypy for SDK users.

anti_patterns: Defining new operation classes or methods in __init__.py or _client.py — all operations must come from _generated/
Manually editing files under openmeter/_generated/ — overwritten by make gen-api
Importing openmeter.aio in sync/ examples or openmeter (sync) in async/ examples
Committing openmeter/_version.py or openmeter/_commit.py — written transiently by release.sh
Running pyright type checks with mypy — mypy is incompatible with the generated SDK's overloaded constructors

decisions.decision: Client subclasses generated OpenMeterClient rather than wrapping it
decisions.rationale: Subclassing preserves all generated type signatures and avoids proxy boilerplate; wrapping would require re-exporting every method.

decisions.decision: Version computed from live PyPI state rather than git tags in release.sh
decisions.rationale: Allows independent alpha pre-release numbering without coupling to the monorepo tag cadence.

decisions.decision: Pyright only (no mypy) for type checking
decisions.rationale: Generated SDK uses overloaded constructors incompatible with mypy's strict mode; Pyright handles the generated patterns correctly.

code_examples.scenario: Ingesting an event using the async client
code_examples.code:
import asyncio, datetime, uuid
from openmeter.aio import Client
from openmeter.models import Event

async def main():
    async with Client(
        endpoint=os.environ['OPENMETER_ENDPOINT'],
        token=os.environ['OPENMETER_TOKEN'],
    ) as client:
        event = Event(
            id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            source='my-app',
            specversion='1.0',
            type='prompt',
            subject='customer-1',
            time=datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc),
            data={'tokens': 100},
        )
        await client.events.ingest_event(event)

asyncio.run(main())
